themes.hook: For a decade, the 'Corporate-NPA' was the villain of Indian banking. In FY25, the villain has officially changed: It is you, me, and our EMIs.
key_data_points: Retail loans became the top category for loan write-offs by Indian banks for the first time in FY25.
Private lenders are facing a 'CEO shortage' and succession planning failures.
Household leverage is hitting record levels as banks pivot away from corporate debt.
themes.non_obvious_angle: We spent ten years fixing corporate governance, but we ignored the 'governance' of the Indian household. The leadership crunch at major private banks is happening exactly when the risk profile has shifted to the most fragmented and hardest-to-track segment: the retail borrower. We've traded a few massive corporate headaches for millions of tiny, ticking retail ones.
themes.your_take_prompt: Are Indian banks equipped to handle a retail-led credit cycle without the institutional memory of veteran CEOs who are currently exiting the system?

source_articles.title: For the first time, retail top category for loan write-offs by Indian banks in FY25
The great Indian bank CEO shortage: A problem unique to private lenders

themes.hook: You know geopolitics is hitting home not when the Sensex dips, but when your local restaurant stops deep-frying your favorite snack.
key_data_points: 20% surge in ready-to-eat food sales as households face an LPG crunch.
Specialty fertilizer imports are 10-20% costlier due to West Asia tensions.
CRISIL predicts an 8-10% hit to City Gas Distribution (CGD) volumes.
themes.non_obvious_angle: West Asia conflict isn't just an 'energy price' story; it's a 'dietary shift' story. FMCG companies aren't winning because of better marketing; they are winning because the utility grid failed the Indian kitchen. When energy becomes a luxury, consumption moves from 'freshly cooked' to 'processed and shelf-stable' out of sheer necessity.
themes.your_take_prompt: How should FMCG investors view this temporary tailwind in ready-to-eat foods—is it a structural shift or a geopolitical fluke?

source_articles.title: LPG Crunch Fuels 20% Surge in Ready-to-Eat Foods
India’s restaurants stop deep frying food as gas crisis bites
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%

themes.hook: The $250 billion Indian IT engine is facing its 'Nokia moment.' It’s not because we lack talent—it’s because the business model is a math error in the age of GenAI.
key_data_points: Top Indian IT firms are facing sharp market cap declines due to AI disruption.
Data centers are booming, but are becoming a massive strain on the national power grid.
CCI is proactively flagging anti-competitive conduct in the foundational AI layer.
themes.non_obvious_angle: Everyone is worried about AI replacing coders, but the real 'trap' is physical. We are shifting from 'exporting human hours' to 'importing power' to run AI models. The bottleneck for India’s next tech decade isn't the number of engineering graduates; it’s the number of gigawatts we can feed into data centers without collapsing the grid.
themes.your_take_prompt: Can Indian IT pivot from 'labor arbitrage' to 'IP-led services' fast enough to justify their current valuations?

source_articles.title: Top Indian IT firms face sharp market cap declines amid AI disruption
India’s data centre boom: Powering the AI future or straining resources?

themes.hook: 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' just got a pragmatic software update: We’ve realized that to beat the dragon, we might need to let a little bit of its capital into the room.
key_data_points: FDI rules eased for firms with up to 10% Chinese shareholding under the automatic route.
Atomberg is vertically integrating to manufacture indigenous BLDC motors and compressors.
NMDC and GMDC are collaborating on rare earth minerals to cut China dependency.
themes.non_obvious_angle: This isn't a 'softening' of our stance on China; it's a strategic admission of how supply chains actually work. You cannot have vertical integration (like Atomberg) without the initial capital and component expertise that—ironically—often resides in the very supply chains we are trying to replace. We are using Chinese capital to build the infrastructure that will eventually make that capital redundant.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the 10% FDI threshold a 'sweet spot' for growth, or does it invite back-door influence into our strategic sectors?

source_articles.title: India eases FDI rules, allows firms with up to 10% Chinese shareholding
Atomberg expands into component manufacturing, eyes indigenous motors

themes.hook: Forget New York or the NASDAQ. The coolest destination for Indian tech giants in 2024 isn't Silicon Valley—it's Dalal Street.
key_data_points: MakeMyTrip is considering an India listing to tap into the domestic investor base.
Reliance has picked six banks for the massive Jio IPO.
SIP accounts are rising despite high closures, indicating a more 'active' retail base.
themes.non_obvious_angle: We used to think retail investors were 'dumb money' that would flee at the first sign of trouble. The high SIP churn combined with record-high new account growth proves the opposite: Indian retail is becoming tactical. They aren't just 'setting and forgetting'; they are actively reallocating. This depth is what's allowing global giants like MakeMyTrip to finally 'come home' for their valuations.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does the Indian market have the liquidity to absorb a Jio-sized IPO without sucking the air out of the rest of the mid-cap space?

source_articles.title: MakeMyTrip considers India listing to deepen domestic investor base
Reliance Said To Pick Six Banks For Planned Jio IPO
India SIP Accounts Rise Despite High Closures in Feb 2026
